anchor.snippet: A difference engine is an automatic mechanical calculator designed to tabulate polynomial functions
a way to interpolate or tabulate functions by using a small set of polynomial co-efficients
can only add the value of a column n + 1 to column n to produce the new value of n
The engine is programmed by setting initial values to the columns
one iteration (i.e. one full set of addition and carry operations) happens for each rotation of the main shaft
Each iteration creates a new result, and is accomplished in four steps corresponding to four complete turns of the handle
The engine represents negative numbers as ten's complements

annotations.label: Principle is method of divided differences

anchor.snippet: The principle of a difference engine is Newton's method of divided differences
with the goal of tabulating the values
by starting with any polynomial of degree n , the column number n + 1 will always be constant
To tabulate polynomials of degree n , one needs sufficient storage to hold n numbers
The initial values of columns can be calculated by first manually calculating N consecutive values of the function and by backtracking
the initial values can be calculated directly from the constant coefficients
Many commonly used functions are analytic functions , which can be expressed as power series
The Taylor series expresses the function as a sum obtained from its derivatives at one point
Setting 0 as the start of computation we get the simplified Maclaurin series
A solution which guarantees a constant maximum error is to use curve fitting
an N −1th degree polynomial interpolation of the function is found
